title: Comforting Creamy Algerian Peanut Tagine
description: A salty, fluffy, and comforting Algerian dish featuring peanut butter and tadka, perfect for a sick day, and low-carb-friendly.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chicken broth and peanut butter to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
